alt_forms.note: Modern military term for armour plating (vehicles, tanks). Used in miniature game Wikipedia Japan for armoured vehicle rules.

definitions.gloss_en: armour worn by a unit; a protective equipment type that reduces damage taken
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}が{身|み}に{着|つ}ける{防|ぼう}{具|ぐ}；{受|う}けるダメージを{軽|けい}{減|げん}する{装|そう}{備|び}
definitions.explanation_en: Physical armour carried or worn by a unit. Contributes to defensive stats or saving rolls. In TRPGs, may be an equipment item with specific stat modifiers.
definitions.implicit_notes: 鎧 is a classical Japanese word for samurai armour (specifically lamellar/ōyoroi). In game contexts, it generalises to any body armour. The cultural resonance is strong.
definitions.orthography_notes: 鎧 is a native Japanese word for traditional Japanese armour (鎧兜 = armour and helmet). Kotobank dictionary. WH Japan uses アーマーセーブ (armour save) as the saving mechanic term — 鎧 itself is not a WH term. 装甲 is the modern military term for vehicle armour.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In fantasy wargames and TRPGs, 鎧 is the standard armour equipment term. In historical miniature wargames, 装甲 is preferred for mechanised/vehicle units.

usage_jp: {鎧|よろい}を{装|そう}{備|び}する (to equip armour); {鎧|よろい}の{防|ぼう}{御|ぎょ}{値|ち} (armour's defense value)
comparative_notes: アーマーセーブ is WH Japan's specific saving mechanic term — avoid this compound. 鎧 refers to the armour itself and is classical Japanese with no WH concern. 装甲 is preferred for mechanised units.

examples.japanese: {騎|き}{士|し}の{時|じ}{代|だい}には、{彼|かれ}{等|ら}は{剣|けん}での{闘|たたか}いによる{怪|け}{我|が}から{身|み}を{守|まも}るため{鎧|よろい}を{身|み}に{付|つ}けた。
examples.english: In the days of the knights, they wore armor to protect themselves from sword-fight wounds.

cross_references.note: 防御 — defense stat that 鎧 contributes to
貫通力 — penetration; the stat that defeats 鎧
